meta.description: LifeOS Repo/MCP Radar candidate registry. Read-only evaluation data. No external repos cloned, no services activated.

risk_tiers.A0: Read, observe, index, summarize existing accessible content
risk_tiers.A1: Write logs, summaries, low-risk generated notes in designated locations
risk_tiers.A2: Create new files in approved inboxes, staging areas, or generated-output folders
risk_tiers.A3: Modify approved vault or project files according to role and path permissions
risk_tiers.A4: Destructive actions, credential changes, external publishing, financial actions, system-level changes, or permanent deletion
risk_tiers.A5: System mutation: Docker, systemd, iptables, kernel parameters

candidates.name: modelcontextprotocol/servers
candidates.why_interesting: Reference implementations for MCP servers. Foundation for understanding MCP protocol.
candidates.proposed_use: Sandbox-test individual sub-servers (time, fetch, filesystem) to validate MCP integration pattern. Prefer custom LifeOS MCP tools for production.
candidates.reality_check_notes: Blanket 'Sandbox' rating masks individual server risks. Each sub-server needs independent evaluation. Filesystem server has no per-operation allowlist. Git server defaults to write-capable. Memory server persists knowledge graph to disk.

candidates.name: open-webui/mcpo
candidates.why_interesting: Bridges MCP servers to OpenAI-compatible chat UIs. Central MCP routing for Open WebUI and future AI chat interfaces.
candidates.proposed_use: When Open WebUI is deployed, mcpo connects it to LifeOS MCP tools. Scaffold exists at 40_Services/mcpo/. Sandbox-only in V1.
candidates.reality_check_notes: Privilege amplifier, not neutral proxy. No tool-level access control of its own. Hot-reload feature could load new MCP servers at runtime. API key is optional — must be mandatory. Requires MCP servers and AI chat interface that don't exist yet.

candidates.name: github/github-mcp-server
candidates.why_interesting: Official GitHub-maintained MCP server. Deep GitHub integration: repos, issues, PRs, Copilot code review.
candidates.proposed_use: Agent-driven GitHub operations. Read-only status via existing lifeos_services.py is sufficient for V1.
candidates.read_only_mode: flag available but token scope determines effective access
candidates.reality_check_notes: Read-only mode is misleading — PAT scope determines what data is accessible. Remote vs local server modes have different risk profiles. Copilot agent tools can trigger AI code generation. Requires PAT creation (A4-tier action) before any test.

candidates.name: qdrant/mcp-server-qdrant
candidates.why_interesting: Official Qdrant MCP server. Enables AI agents to query and manage vector database collections.
candidates.proposed_use: Semantic search over indexed vault content. Qdrant not deployed; infrastructure gap too large for V1.
candidates.reality_check_notes: Write-capable by default (QDRANT_READ_ONLY=false). Runtime downloads ML model from Hugging Face at first use — supply-chain risk. QDRANT_LOCAL_PATH grants direct disk access. No Qdrant, no indices, no embedding pipeline. Upgraded from 'defer' to 'reject' — infrastructure gap is too large.

candidates.name: microsoft/playwright-mcp
candidates.why_interesting: Powerful browser automation by the official Playwright team.
candidates.proposed_use: None in V1. Browser automation already rejected by MCP Security Policy.
candidates.read_only_mode: not a security boundary per Microsoft's own docs
candidates.reality_check_notes: Permanently rejected. Microsoft: 'Playwright MCP is NOT a security boundary.' browser_run_code_unsafe is RCE-equivalent. CDP endpoint hijacking. Persistent browser profiles store cookies and sessions. --allowed-origins does not block redirects. Docker mode requires --no-sandbox.

candidates.name: microsoft/markitdown
candidates.why_interesting: Converts PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, EPUB, ZIP, images, audio to Markdown. Microsoft-maintained.
candidates.proposed_use: Capture Processor — convert captured documents to Markdown for vault import. Not an MCP tool. Reclassified to Capture Processor roadmap.
candidates.read_only_mode: convert_local() available; cloud backends must be disabled
candidates.reality_check_notes: NOT an MCP server — it's a Python library. Community MCP wrappers add supply-chain risk. Cloud backends (Azure, OpenAI) exfiltrate data. Plugin system = arbitrary code execution. Most genuinely useful candidate in the list — evaluate as Capture Processor, not MCP tool.

candidates.name: OpenHands/openhands
candidates.why_interesting: Full AI-powered software engineering platform. Agent Canvas for multi-agent coding.
candidates.proposed_use: Sandboxed agent lab — not main LifeOS executor. Reclassified from MCP Radar to AI Agent Infrastructure.
candidates.reality_check_notes: NOT an MCP server — it's a coding agent platform that consumes MCP. Docker socket requirement conflicts with LifeOS permanent prohibition. LLM API key required. Every LifeOS constraint in Sandbox_Policy.md is aspirational — none implemented or tested. Most elaborate fantasy in the candidate list.

candidates.name: kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox
candidates.why_interesting: Kubernetes-native AI sandbox with strong isolation (gVisor, Kata Containers).
candidates.proposed_use: None in current architecture. LifeOS uses Docker Compose. Kubernetes is explicitly deferred.
candidates.reality_check_notes: NOT an MCP server — Kubernetes operator that requires a Kubernetes cluster. LifeOS runs on Docker Compose. Kubernetes explicitly deferred. Deploying minikube/kind would consume 2-4GB RAM and 20GB+ disk at 91% usage. Remove from MCP Radar entirely.

candidates.name: punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers
candidates.why_interesting: Community-curated list of hundreds of MCP servers. Broad ecosystem awareness.
candidates.proposed_use: Awareness tool — discover what MCP servers exist. NEVER install based on list presence alone.
candidates.reality_check_notes: Useful for awareness, dangerous as authority. No security review. Anyone can submit a PR. Includes servers LifeOS has already rejected. 2,400+ open PRs. Promotes commercial third-party registry.

candidates.name: tolkonepiu/best-of-mcp-servers
candidates.why_interesting: Ranked, categorized list of 400+ MCP servers with structured YAML data and quality scores.
candidates.proposed_use: Structured discovery source — use projects.yaml for programmatic candidate identification. Build LifeOS-specific scoring.
candidates.reality_check_notes: Structured data is genuinely useful for programmatic discovery. Quality scores are actively dangerous — measure GitHub popularity, not LifeOS safety. #1 ranked server (Playwright MCP) is permanently rejected.

catalog_groups._note: This catalog combines the 10 Repo Radar candidates below with 10 additional entries from the older MCP_Candidate_Catalog.md. Entries marked with (existing catalog) come from the older catalog only and are not in the candidates array above.
high_risk_quarantine: microsoft/markitdown (as MCP server; reclassify to Capture Processor)
